summary: @wagni_bot/eth-agent postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys, crypto wallets, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js recursively scans the current working directory, the user's home directory, and known crypto wallet locations (.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, Library/Ethereum) for files matching wallet/keystore/seed/mnemonic/private/.pem/.key/id_rsa patterns. It also reads private keys from ~/.ssh/, regex-matches Ethereum/Bitcoin private keys and BIP-39 seed words in file contents, and filters process.env for variables containing PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS. The collected contents (truncated to 10KB per file), along with hostname, username, and cwd, are POSTed over plain HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial eth-agent SDK' but ships no SDK functionality — only the credential-stealing postinstall — indicating a lure targeting Ethereum developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
